Incredible stuff. I've spent three hours trying to fix a compatibility issue with the new update, only to find out the license key expired. Now, to top it all off, my battery just died and the charger isn't being recognized. Can we just roll back the update or do I need a new laptop?

Incredible stuff. I've spent three hours trying to fix a compatibility issue with the new update, only to find out the license key expired. Now, to top it all off, my battery just died and the charger isn't being recognized. Can we just roll back the update or do I need a new laptop?

Deep Reasoning
The ticket was routed to Hardware Support because the most critical barrier to operation is a physical power failure that prevents the device from being turned on. These symptoms, specifically the unrecognized charger and dead battery, require hardware diagnostics or component replacement before any software issues can be addressed.

Wait, now the portal is saying 'Forbidden 403' but my colleague can get in just fine. Is it my computer or the internet? Anyway, the buzzing is really loud now, hope it doesn't explode lol

I hate to be the bearer of bad news, but it appears our 'secure' firewall has decided to take an unscheduled vacation. I’m seeing several strange IP addresses from overseas trying to handshake with the main database server 'DB-PROD-01'. It would be absolutely lovely if someone could investigate before our data starts living elsewhere. Just a thought

However, the context of a user reporting this screenshot suggests the problem is related to **browse abandonment**. The image and title of the blog post indicate the user is likely experiencing or investigating issues where potential customers are adding items to their cart but not completing the purchase, and are seeking strategies to address this. My laptop randomly freezes for 5-10 seconds every few minutes, the fans spin up to max, Task Manager shows nothing above 15% CPU, and it only happens when connected to a second monitor via DisplayPort. Unplugging the monitor immediately fixes it. This started after a Windows Update last Tuesday.

Outlook is sending emails but they arrive with a 4-hour delay. This only happens when emailing external domains (Gmail, Yahoo), internal emails are instant. The sent folder shows the email sent immediately. IT checked Exchange logs and they look normal.

Getting BSOD DRIVER_IRQL_NOT_LESS_OR_EQUAL with stop code 0x000000D1 on ndis.sys. Only crashes during Zoom calls when screen sharing. Normal video calls are fine. Minidump shows fwpkclnt.sys in the call stack.

VPN connects successfully (shows IP change confirmed), but I can't reach internal servers by hostname — only by IP address. nslookup company.internal returns Non-existent domain. ipconfig /all shows my DNS is still pointing to 8.8.8.8 after VPN connect
OneDrive keeps crashing at startup. I've reinstalled it 3 times, cleared the cache, and ran sfc /scannow which found nothing. See attached screenshot from Event Viewer.

SQL Server Management Studio throws this error when I try to connect to our dev database. It works on my colleague's machine with the same credentials. Firewall is disabled on both machines and port 1433 is confirmed open

My Wi-Fi disconnects only on Tuesdays and Thursdays between 2-3 PM. Works perfectly every other time. Using a laptop, two other people in the office have the same laptop and same issue at the exact same times. Internet from mobile hotspot works fine during outages.

Printer works when I'm in the office. When WFH on VPN, it shows 'Ready' in the queue but jobs disappear without printing. Printer is network-shared. I can ping the printer's IP. The print spooler service is running.
